company.what_they_do: Return is an Amsterdam-based, independent large-scale battery energy storage provider founded in 2021. It builds, owns, and operates grid-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS) and offers them as a service ('storage-as-a-service') to a range of customers: utilities/grid operators, large industrial energy consumers (factories, data centers, logistics companies), and energy traders/financials. Its model is strictly infrastructure — it does not trade energy itself. Instead, it leases battery capacity to customers who decide how to use it. A 'virtual battery' concept lets clients access a network of battery projects rather than a single site. Return operates a smart technology platform providing real-time insight into where energy is available and needed across its pan-European network. It also has a sub-brand, Return Industrial Solutions (RIS), focused on 'behind-the-grid connection' storage for large industrial clients with existing grid infrastructure. Current markets: Netherlands, Belgium, Germany, Spain, with active expansion into new markets.
raw_notes: Founded 2021; founders cited as Arno Hendriks, Willem-Jan Schutte, and Daniel Witvoet (Seedtable). Willem-Jan Schutte is Founder & CEO (confirmed in APG/EU-Startups press). Sjoerd Bazen is Managing Director.
Headquartered in Amsterdam (HNK Amsterdam Schinkel, Anthony Fokkerweg 1, 1059 CM).
Self-described as 'Europe's leading independent large-scale energy storage provider'; 100+ professionals.
Operates 70 MW of storage capacity; 450 MW under construction (as of Oct 2025). Goal: 5 GW total power output by 2030.
Over €2 billion in long-term customer contracts secured.
Major funding: €300 million investment from APG (on behalf of pension fund ABP) closed Oct 2025 for a minority stake — one of the largest European BESS financings of 2025.

Key projects: Antares (100 MW/200 MWh, Waddinxveen, NL, connected to TenneT, expected mid-2026); Sirius (220 kV extra high voltage, Meeden, NL); Spica (220 kV, Meeden, NL); Brietlingen (Germany, via J&P Batterieprojekte acquisition 2024); Orion portfolio (three projects, Vizcaya, Spain); Star (Terneuzen, NL, operational since Nov 2021).
Also investor in Lion Storage / Mufasa (1.4 GWh BESS, Vlissingen, NL; Tesla as EPC/BESS provider).
Partnered with Bessmart (Berlin) for 670 MWh of German BESS by 2027.
Key commercial partnerships: Vattenfall (8-year capacity offtake agreement for Antares/Waddinxveen); project financing via ING, Meewind, Nationaal Groenfonds.
Previously operated under 'SemperPower' brand; rebranded to Return in early 2025.
Return Industrial Solutions (RIS): behind-the-meter/behind-grid-connection storage for energy-intensive industry; supports decarbonization and cost competitiveness.
Neutral/independent positioning is a core differentiator: does not trade energy itself; serves all sides of the energy chain (grid operators, producers, consumers, traders).
Core value proposition per homepage: anticipates customer needs, unites stakeholders, enables net-zero energy system, storage-as-a-service without capex or operational risk for customers.
Founded by minds behind Sunrock and Stecc Capital (FoundersToday, Feb 2024).
